metadata.title:
MAYOR PUTS LABOR ISSUE


Up TO CITY COUNCIL

Two Ordinances to Enforce
Scale aHd Bar Alien


Employees.

pages.raw_text:
Councilman Funk on Tuesday
evening will introduce two new or
dinances that embody Mayor
Pratt's ideas on the city labor ques-
ton. The council will either have
to pass them or go on record
against the policy most popular
with labor interests.


The first ordinance. In addition
to binding the city, contractors and
subcontractors doing city work, to
the eight hour day and $2.75 scale,
with time and a half for overtime,
provides for incorporation In the
contract of an agreement that pay
books will be kept open to inspec-
ton by the board Of works. Either
this or verified copies of the pay
rolls must be submitted to the
board on demand. The mayor now
participates In the deliberations of
the board OF works, a display of in
dustry and personal interest in the
everyday affairs OF the city here
tofore foreign to the executive of
flee.


The penalty for violation of this
ordinance has been fixed at from
$25 to 5000. coming under the $500
classification of cases that can be
tried In the police court.


The second ordinance Is designed
to guarantee city work to citizens,
1t provides that all public work
must be done by citizens of the
United States and that citizens of
the state Of Washington and city
OF Spokane be given preference.
The only time In which alien labor
will be permitted under this Or
dinance is when It is found impos-
sible to obtain citizen labor.


There have been numerous reso-
buttons on these subjects passed
by the council, and the committee
Of the whole boneyard might d.
vulge an ordinance or two that has
been embalmed instead of born, but
the mayors measures will put the
whole thing on a clear, practical
basis that will permit of no dodg-
Ing the responsibilities the coun-
Gil passes them.


II the council does not II will
experience the necessity of making
large and luminous explanation not
only to labor interests but to nu-
merous taxpayers who object to
paying their money for alien labor
when people who maintain families
in this city are unable to find en-
ployment.
